---
title: "compare_four_models_cog_function_matched"
author: "Hui Xin Ng"
date: "2023-07-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
source("R/load_brainage_df.R")
source("R/load_merged_df.R")
source("R/helper_functions.R")
source(".Rprofile")


# renv::init()
# renv::restore()
 # renv::snapshot(type = "implicit")
renv::status()
renv::restore()
renv::status()

# ---- Load packages for current session ---- # (analysis scripts should ONLY do this part)
 
#invisible(lapply(pkgs, library, character.only = TRUE))
# ---- Sanity check: all packages available ---- 

#stopifnot(all(vapply(pkgs, requireNamespace, logical(1), quietly = TRUE)))
```
   

```{r}
# =========================
# Data import / pre processing
# =========================

# Read source data
path <- file.path(
  "..", "data_original",
  "Master_BAI_080720_with_051517completers_060117imagingmaster_genotypes_brainages_ENIGMA_FS_MERGEFK_30122020.sav"
)

original_df <- haven::read_sav(path)
original_df
original_df <- original_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    BDI = ifelse(dxgroupFK == 0, 0, BDI))

# Keep only variables used downstream
original_df <- original_df %>%
  dplyr::select(
    BDI,
    agevisit,
    dxgroupFK,

    # DKEFS Trails
    trvsoerw,   # visual scanning omission errors
    trvscerw,   # visual scanning commission errors
    trnsctrw,   # number sequencing completion time (TMT A)
    trlnctrw,   # letter-number sequencing completion time (TMT B)
    trmsctrw,   # motor speed completion time

    # DKEFS Color-Word Interference
    cwicnrw,    # color naming raw
    cwiwrrw,    # word reading raw
    cwiirw,     # inhibition raw
    cwiisrw,    # inhibition/switching raw
    gender,

    
    # Clinical measures
    scid_a1,    # current GAF
    panspos,    # PANSS positive
    pansneg,    # PANSS negative
    pansgen,    # PANSS general
    ymrstot,
    hamtot17,
    hamtot28,
    HistoryPsychosis,

    visit,
    stdyptid
  )

# Remove NAs within each participant, then keep baseline only
# Note: this preserves your original approach exactly
original_df <- setDT(original_df)[, lapply(.SD, na.omit), by = stdyptid] 
original_df <- original_df %>% filter(visit == 0) %>% mutate(visit = 'Baseline') 


```


# Perform matching to create matched_df
```{r}
# =========================
# Simple age matching (no extra packages)
# =========================

# Split groups
bd <- merged_df %>% dplyr::filter(dxgroupFK == 1)   # BD
hc <- merged_df %>% dplyr::filter(dxgroupFK == 0)   # HC

# Sort by age (helps stability)
bd <- bd %>% arrange(agevisit)
hc <- hc %>% arrange(agevisit)


caliper <- 3  # age tolerance
matched_pairs <- list()
used_hc <- rep(FALSE, nrow(hc))

for (i in seq_len(nrow(bd))) {
  age_bd <- bd$agevisit[i]
  gender_bd <- bd$gender[i]
  
  # Only match within same gender
  valid <- hc$gender == gender_bd
  
  diffs <- abs(hc$agevisit - age_bd)
  diffs[!valid | used_hc] <- Inf
  
  j <- which.min(diffs)
  
  if (is.finite(diffs[j]) && diffs[j] <= caliper) {
    matched_pairs[[length(matched_pairs) + 1]] <- rbind(bd[i, ], hc[j, ])
    used_hc[j] <- TRUE
  }
}

matched_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(matched_pairs)

# Combine matched dataset
matched_df <- do.call(rbind, matched_pairs)

prop.table(table(matched_df$dxgroupFK, matched_df$gender), margin = 1)

for (df in list(matched_df, merged_df)) {
 # print(plot_age_density(df))
  print(run_gender_chisq(df))
  print(run_age_wilcox(df))
}

```

### Figures for matched and unmatched samples
```{r}

bind_rows(
  merged_df %>% mutate(dataset = "Unmatched"),
  matched_df %>% mutate(dataset = "Matched")
) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = agevisit, fill = factor(dxgroupFK))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  facet_wrap(~ dataset) +
  labs(
    title = "Age Distribution Before vs After Matching",
    x = "Age",
    y = "Density",
    fill = "Group (0 = HC, 1 = BD)"
  ) + theme_classic()

bind_rows(
  merged_df %>% mutate(dataset = "Unmatched"),
  matched_df %>% mutate(dataset = "Matched")
) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(dxgroupFK), fill = factor(gender))) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  facet_wrap(~ dataset) +
  labs(
    title = "Gender Proportions Before vs After Matching",
    x = "Group (0 = HC, 1 = BD)",
    y = "Proportion",
    fill = "Gender"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```


### Data cleaning for cognitive variablesS
```{r}

# Cognitive test variables
cog_vars <- c(
  "trvsoerw", # DKEFS - Trails Visual Scanning Omission Errors Raw (Misses)
  "trvscerw", # DKEFS - Trails Visual Scanning Commission Errors Raw (False Alarm)
  "trnsctrw", # DKEFS - Trails Number Sequencing Completion Times Raw (TMT A)
  "trlnctrw", # DKEFS - Trails Letter-Number Completion Times Raw (TMT B)
  "trmsctrw", # DKEFS - Trails Motor Speed Completion Times Raw
  "cwicnrw",  # DKEFS - Color Word Interference - Color Naming Raw
  "cwiwrrw",  # DKEFS - Color Word Interference - Word Reading Raw
  "cwiirw",   # DKEFS - Color Word Interference - Inhibition Raw
  "cwiisrw",  # DKEFS - Color Word Interference - Inhibition/Switching Raw
 
  "dxgroupFK",
  "gender")


cog_test_scores_merged <-merged_df %>% dplyr::select(cog_vars)
cog_test_scores_matched <-matched_df %>% dplyr::select(cog_vars)

# =========================================================
# Remove outliers from cognitive scores
# Using the helper function defined earlier
# =========================================================

outliers_removed_merged  <- remove_sd_outliers(cog_test_scores_merged,   n_sd = 2)
outliers_removed_matched <- remove_sd_outliers(cog_test_scores_matched, n_sd = 2)


# =========================================================
# Plot cleaned score distributions for MERGED dataset
# Exclude BD051 as in your earlier QC step
# =========================================================


outliers_removed_merged %>%
  dplyr::filter(stdyptid != "BD051") %>%
  reshape2::melt() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value, fill = factor(dxgroupFK))) +
  geom_histogram() +
  facet_wrap(~ variable, scales = "free") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "Distributions of Cognitive Scores After Outlier Removal (Merged)",
    x = "Score",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Group"
  )


# =========================================================
# Plot cleaned score distributions for MATCHED dataset
# =========================================================

outliers_removed_matched %>%
  dplyr::filter(stdyptid != "BD051") %>%
  reshape2::melt() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value, fill = factor(dxgroupFK))) +
  geom_histogram() +
  facet_wrap(~ variable, scales = "free") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "Distributions of Cognitive Scores After Outlier Removal (Matched)",
    x = "Score",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Group"
  )


# =========================================================
# Check normality of numeric variables after outlier removal
# Returns Shapiro-Wilk p-values by variable
# =========================================================

normality_merged <- outliers_removed_merged %>%
  dplyr::select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  test_normality()

normality_matched <- outliers_removed_matched %>%
  dplyr::select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  test_normality()

normality_merged
normality_matched


# =========================================================
# Run one-way ANOVA for each cognitive test in MERGED dataset
# Group comparison: HC vs BD
# =========================================================

anova_results_merged <- outliers_removed_merged %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(stdyptid, gender, dxgroupFK),
    names_to = "test",
    values_to = "score"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(score)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(score = round(score, 2)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(test) %>%
  do(broom::tidy(aov(score ~ factor(dxgroupFK), data = .))) %>%
  dplyr::filter(term == "factor(dxgroupFK)") %>%
  dplyr::select(test, statistic, p.value, df) %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    Test = test,
    F_value = statistic,
    p_value = p.value,
    Df = df
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(p_value)

anova_results_merged


# =========================================================
# Run one-way ANOVA for each cognitive test in MATCHED dataset
# =========================================================

anova_results_matched <- outliers_removed_matched %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(stdyptid, gender, dxgroupFK),
    names_to = "test",
    values_to = "score"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(score)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(score = round(score, 2)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(test) %>%
  do(broom::tidy(aov(score ~ factor(dxgroupFK), data = .))) %>%
  dplyr::filter(term == "factor(dxgroupFK)") %>%
  dplyr::select(test, statistic, p.value, df) %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    Test = test,
    F_value = statistic,
    p_value = p.value,
    Df = df
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(p_value)

anova_results_matched


# =========================================================
# Boxplots of cleaned cognitive scores for MERGED dataset
# Useful for compact visual group comparison
# =========================================================

outliers_removed_merged %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(stdyptid, dxgroupFK, gender),
    names_to = "test",
    values_to = "score"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(dxgroupFK), y = score, fill = factor(dxgroupFK))) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ test, scales = "free") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "Cognitive Scores by Group (Merged)",
    x = "Group",
    y = "Cognitive Score",
    fill = "Group"
  )


# =========================================================
# Boxplots of cleaned cognitive scores for MATCHED dataset
# =========================================================

outliers_removed_matched %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(stdyptid, dxgroupFK, gender),
    names_to = "test",
    values_to = "score"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(dxgroupFK), y = score, fill = factor(dxgroupFK))) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ test, scales = "free") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "Cognitive Scores by Group (Matched)",
    x = "Group",
    y = "Cognitive Score",
    fill = "Group"
  )


```
